observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) expedite the updating of taxpayers’ index cards in order to provide the Accounting Office an updated Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due as the basis in setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts; (ii) strictly enforce the remedies for the collection of delinquent RPT as expressly provided in Section 256 of R.A. No. 7160; and (iii) institute appropriate strategies to further improve the collection efficiency; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to use the updated Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due as the basis in recording the RPT/SET tax Receivables at the beginning of the year; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records of RPT/SET Receivables in order to resolve the disparity of ₱64,186,694.06 and to report reliably the said accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Updating of taxpayers’ index cards in order to provide the Accounting Office an updated Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due as the basis in setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts;
Issuance of notice of delinquency to delinquent taxpayers of real property in different barangays, posting of notice of delinquency for real property taxes at designated areas in different barangays of the Municipality.
Conduct of tax information campaign thru attendance in general assemblies and meetings.
b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to use the updated Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due as the basis in recording the RPT/SET tax Receivables at the beginning of the year; and
c) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records of RPT/SET Receivables in order to resolve the disparity of ₱64,186,694.06 and to report reliably the said accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE require the (a) Inventory Committee and Supply Officer to (i) conduct complete physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count for CY 2023 and immediately submit the related reports to the Audit Team; and (ii) reconcile the resultant inventory reports with the accounting records; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELCs for the proper control and monitoring of the agency’s property and equipment; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to consider the guidelines and procedures on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information. We also recommended that the Disposal Committee facilitate the disposal of the noted unserviceable PPEs in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Inventory Committee and Supply Officer to (i) conduct complete physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count for CY 2023 and immediately submit the related reports to the Audit Team; and (ii) reconcile the resultant inventory reports with the accounting records;
(b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELCs for the proper control and monitoring of the agency’s property and equipment; and
(c) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to consider the guidelines and procedures on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information.
Disposal Committee to complete the IIRUP and proceed with disposal in accordance with the guidelines set.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) perform verification and reconciliation of balances of Due to LGUs account of the Municipality with the Due from LGUs account of the Provincial Government to establish the correct and accurate balances of both accounts; and (ii) draw the necessary journal entries to reflect the corrected/adjusted balances of Due to LGUs; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) initiate the regular/timely release of the quarterly RPT shares of all Barangays of the Municipality, pursuant to Section 271(d) of R.A. 7160; (ii) equitably allocate the deduction of P150,000.00 spent for the catering services during the Papayanos Night from the RPT shares of 13 barangays; and (iii) stop the practice of spending the unreleased RPT shares of barangays at the municipal level.
observations.managementAction: (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) perform verification and reconciliation of balances of Due to LGUs account of the Municipality with the Due from LGUs account of the Provincial Government to establish the correct and accurate balances of both accounts; and (ii) draw the necessary journal entries to reflect the corrected/adjusted balances of Due to LGUs; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) initiate the regular/timely release of the quarterly RPT shares of all Barangays of the Municipality, pursuant to Section 271(d) of R.A. 7160; (ii) equitably allocate the deduction of P150,000.00 spent for the catering services during the Papayanos Night from the RPT shares of 13 barangays; and (iii) stop the practice of spending the unreleased RPT shares of barangays at the municipal level.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) recognize and make necessary adjustment in the Employee Benefits account, update/reconcile employee leave credits and perform other relevant activities within three (3) years from the effectivity of COA Circular No. 2024-009; (b) charge the payments for monetization of leave credits to Terminal Leave Benefits account pursuant to Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009; and (c) ensure that the disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: (a) recognize and make necessary adjustment in the Employee Benefits account, update/reconcile employee leave credits and perform other relevant activities within three (3) years from the effectivity of COA Circular No. 2024-009; (b) charge the payments for monetization of leave credits to Terminal Leave Benefits account pursuant to Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009; and (c) ensure that the disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) coordination of the BPLO, Municipal Treasurer and Sangguniang Bayan to (i) regularly review and update the Revenue Code at least every five years, as mandated by law, in order to optimize its revenue generation for the benefit of its constituents; (ii) ensure that the amended Revenue Code reflects the current economic conditions and complies with the Local Government Code of 1991; (b) BPLO to (i) be more strict in the implementation and enforcement of the Municipal Revenue Code; (ii) use the declared gross sale in the application form as basis in the initial computation of business tax; and (iii) require the business tax payers to submit a copy of ITR for the proper computation of deficiency tax, if any; (b) Municipal Treasurer to examine and verify the books of accounts and other pertinent records of any business establishments; and (c) BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to demand the remittance of computed under collection of business tax and deficiency tax totaling ₱7,885.19. We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to implement measures to enhance the efficiency of business tax collection for increased local revenue.
observations.managementAction: (a) coordination of the BPLO, Municipal Treasurer and Sangguniang Bayan to (i) regularly review and update the Revenue Code at least every five years, as mandated by law, in order to optimize its revenue generation for the benefit of its constituents; (ii) ensure that the amended Revenue Code reflects the current economic conditions and complies with the Local Government Code of 1991; (b) BPLO to (i) be more strict in the implementation and enforcement of the Municipal Revenue Code; (ii) use the declared gross sale in the application form as basis in the initial computation of business tax; and (iii) require the business tax payers to submit a copy of ITR for the proper computation of deficiency tax, if any; (b) Municipal Treasurer to examine and verify the books of accounts and other pertinent records of any business establishments; and (c) BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to demand the remittance of computed under collection of business tax and deficiency tax totaling ₱7,885.19.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), through the GAD Focal Person to (a) continue its efforts of improving gender mainstreaming to achieve full compliance with the provisions of relevant laws and regulations on GAD; (b) employ the HGDG in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD; and (c) ensure the complete implementation of activities indicated in the GPB to maximize the intervention in addressing gender-issues in the Municipality. We also recommended that the (a) GFPS to strengthen its planning process; and the (b) Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the expenditures are within the limits of the budget through proper control and monitoring.
observations.managementAction:
(a) continue its efforts of improving gender mainstreaming to achieve full compliance with the provisions of relevant laws and regulations on GAD; (b) employ the HGDG in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD; and (c) ensure the complete implementation of activities indicated in the GPB to maximize the intervention in addressing gender-issues in the Municipality.
GFPS to strengthen its planning process; and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the expenditures are within the limits of the budget through proper control and monitoring.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed that the (a) OIC - Municipal Accountant continue complying with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes on compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from government money payments; and (b) OIC - Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer exert extra effort in the processing and timely recording of disbursements in order to fully remit the taxes withheld within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: (a) OIC - Municipal Accountant continue complying with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes on compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from government money payments; and (b) OIC - Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer exert extra effort in the processing and timely recording of disbursements in order to fully remit the taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) adopt effective cash management strategies with the end view of gradually reducing the cash deficit without sacrificing the funding of current operations and due and demandable obligations; and (ii) institute effective measures and strategies to improve and maximize local revenue collections; and (b) Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, to review thoroughly the amount of estimated revenue in the formulation of Annual Budget so that the target receipts can be set under realistic conditions and attainable level.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records of RPT and SET Receivables in order to report reliably the said accounts in the financial statements; (b) Municipal Assessor to submit the monthly assessment roll as well as other reports needed to update the Certified List of RPT Delinquencies to the Municipal Treasurer; and (c) Municipal Treasurer to strictly enforce the remedies for the collection of delinquent RPT as expressly provided in Section 256 of R.A. No. 7160 and institute appropriate strategies to further improve the collection efficiency.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE require the (a) Inventory Committee and Supply Officer to (i) conduct complete physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count for CY 2024 and immediately submit the complete RPCPPE to the Audit Team; and (ii) reconcile the resultant inventory reports with the accounting records; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELCs for the proper control and monitoring of the agency’s property and equipment; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to consider the guidelines and procedures on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information.

We also recommended that the Disposal Committee facilitate the disposal of the noted unserviceable PPEs in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to (i) make proper coordination on timely determination of the completion of infrastructure projects in order to subsequently facilitate the necessary recording/transfer to the appropriate asset accounts for fair presentation of balances in the financial statements; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) record the necessary adjusting entries as mentioned above; (ii) compute and record the related Depreciation Expense and Accumulated Depreciation reckoning from the date of completion of the projects; and (iii) strictly comply with the recording requirements provided in Section 50, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) continue its efforts in the timely implementation of PPAs and full utilization of the funds transferred by National Government Agencies (NGAs); and (b) require the Heads of Offices concerned to strictly observe the time frame of implementation/completion of the on-going projects for the efficient delivery of public services to the intended beneficiaries.

We also recommended that the LCE direct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC, to immediately submit the noted lacking documentary requirements on the ₱2,737,374.44 disbursements; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) reclassify from GF books to TF books the BFP fire code fees received in CY 2024; and (ii) make a transfer of deposit to TF bank account.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) perform verification and reconciliation of balances of Due to LGUs account of the Municipality with the Due from LGUs account of the Provincial Government to establish the correct and accurate balances of both accounts; and (ii) draw the necessary journal entries to reflect the corrected/adjusted balances of Due to LGUs; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) initiate the regular/timely release of the quarterly RPT shares of all Barangays of the Municipality, pursuant to Section 271(d) of R.A. 7160; (ii) equitably allocate the deduction of P150,000.00 spent for the catering services during the Papayanos Night from the RPT shares of 13 barangays; and (iii) stop the practice of spending the unreleased RPT shares of barangays at the municipal level.

We recommended that Management: (a) recognize and make necessary adjustment in the Employee Benefits account, update/reconcile employee leave credits and perform other relevant activities within three (3) years from the effectivity of COA Circular No. 2024-009; (b) charge the payments for monetization of leave credits to Terminal Leave Benefits account pursuant to Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009; and (c) ensure that the disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) coordination of the BPLO, Municipal Treasurer and Sangguniang Bayan to (i) regularly review and update the Revenue Code at least every five years, as mandated by law, in order to optimize its revenue generation for the benefit of its constituents; (ii) ensure that the amended Revenue Code reflects the current economic conditions and complies with the Local Government Code of 1991; (b) BPLO to (i) be more strict in the implementation and enforcement of the Municipal Revenue Code; (ii) use the declared gross sale in the application form as basis in the initial computation of business tax; and (iii) require the business tax payers to submit a copy of ITR for the proper computation of deficiency tax, if any; (b) Municipal Treasurer to examine and verify the books of accounts and other pertinent records of any business establishments; and (c) BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to demand the remittance of computed under collection of business tax and deficiency tax totaling ₱7,885.19.

We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to implement measures to enhance the efficiency of business tax collection for increased local revenue.

We recommended that the Local Development Council (LDC), headed by the LCE to continue to plan and effectively implement priority developmental projects allowing to flow essential benefits to constituents.

We also recommended that the LCE direct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC, to immediately submit the noted lacking documentary requirements; (b) BAC to require the contractors to put up warranty security in order to protect the Municipality from potential costs, or worse, infrastructural failure; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to compute the contractors’ retention money based on the gross amount of progress payments.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) LDRRMO, in collaboration with LDRRMC to ensure the optimal utilization of LDRRMF through a more realistic project planning and budgeting; (b) BAC and OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit all the requisite documents, and henceforth ensure full compliance with R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; (c) Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to establish effective measures expediting the process of DRRM disbursements in order to fix the unexpended QRF and MF – MOOE balances of the current year and transfer them to the Special Trust Fund at the end of the year; and (d) OIC - Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry at year-end to recognize the unexpended balance as Trust Liability – DRRMF in the financial statements, as illustrated in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), through the GAD Focal Person to (a) continue its efforts of improving gender mainstreaming to achieve full compliance with the provisions of relevant laws and regulations on GAD; (b) employ the HGDG in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD; and (c) ensure the complete implementation of activities indicated in the GPB to maximize the intervention in addressing gender-issues in the Municipality.

We also recommended that the (a) GFPS to strengthen its planning process; and the (b) Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the expenditures are within the limits of the budget through proper control and monitoring.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed that the (a) OIC - Municipal Accountant continue complying with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes on compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from government money payments; and (b) OIC - Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer exert extra effort in the processing and timely recording of disbursements in order to fully remit the taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue complying with the R.A. No. 8291 or GSIS Act of 1997 in the withholding of premiums and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to GSIS.

We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue complying with the R.A. No. 9679 or HDMF law of 2009 on the withholding of contributions and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to HDMF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue complying with the R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos. 9241 and 10606 or the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 on the withholding of contributions from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to PHIC.

There were no audit suspensions, disallowances and charges issued during the Calendar Year 2024. The Municipal Government neither had unsettled balance of audit suspensions, disallowances and charges as at year-end.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to make proper coordination on timely determination of the completion of infrastructure projects in order to subsequently facilitate the necessary recording/transfer to the appropriate asset accounts for fair presentation of balances in the financial statements; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) record the necessary adjusting entries as mentioned above; (ii) compute and record the related Depreciation Expense and Accumulated Depreciation reckoning from the date of completion of the projects; and (iii) strictly comply with the recording requirements provided in Section 50, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Development Council (LDC), headed by the LCE to continue to plan and effectively implement priority developmental projects allowing to flow essential benefits to constituents. We also recommended that the LCE direct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC, to immediately submit the noted lacking documentary requirements; (b) BAC to require the contractors to put up warranty security in order to protect the Municipality from potential costs, or worse, infrastructural failure; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to compute the contractors’ retention money based on the gross amount of progress payments.

observations.recommendation: No recommendation (informational).

observations.managementAction:
The MTO is in the process of updating the taxpayers’ index cards.







Posting of Notices of Delinquency in the Municipal Hall Bulletin Board and in different barangays in the municipality was done by the MTO.

The Municipal Engineer submitted an updated layout plan of the public market.

Contracts were issued to market stall owners.
Issued demand letters to delinquent stall owners.

observations.recommendation:
For the deficiencies discussed above, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE require the 
(a) Inventory Committee and Property Officer to 
(i) conduct complete physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count for CY 2022 and immediately submit the related reports to the Audit Team; and 
(ii) reconcile the resultant inventory reports with the accounting records and immediately settle the variance therein; 
(b) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to 
(i) maintain complete PPE records such as PPELCs and PCs for the proper control and monitoring of the agency property and equipment; and 


(ii) consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information.

We further recommended that the 
OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) complete the listing/schedule of each PPE item to support the general ledger balances of the PPE in the financial statements; and (b) compute/adjust the depreciation expense based on each item of PPE and with residual value of 10% pursuant to Section 4, Chapter 2 of NGAS for LGUs Manual.
observations.managementAction: Creation of GSO in the CY 2024 Annual Budget in order to perform the functions delegated to MTO as regards PPE of the municipality

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE direct the 
(a) LDRRMO, in collaboration with LDRRMC, to 
(i) update the LDRRMP in accordance with Item 3.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1; and 
(ii) to transmit the pictures taken during the distribution of the relief goods; 
(b) LDRRMO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to comply with the reporting for LDRRM activities as provided for under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and COA Circular No. 2012-002; 

(c) Acting Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to establish effective measures expediting the process of DRRM disbursements in order (i) to fix the unexpended QRF and MF – MOOE balances of the current year and (ii) to transfer them to a special trust fund at the end of the year; and 
(d)  OIC - Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry at year-end to recognize the unexpended balance as Trust Liability – DRRMF in the financial statements as illustrated in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the LCE direct the 
(a) Human Resources and Management Officer III to 
(i) submit regularly to the Audit Team the copies of Contracts for Job Order (JO) and Contract of Service (COS) workers within the prescribed period as required by COA Circular No. 2009-001; and 
(ii) implement control measures on submission of Accomplishment Reports (Ars) of JO/COS Personnel; and 
(b) OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure completeness of supporting documents to substantiate the payroll claims.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to institute effective measures and strategies to improve and maximize local revenue collections; and (b) henceforth, Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Budget Officer, to review thoroughly the amount of estimated revenue in the formulation of Annual Budget so that the target receipts can be set under realistic conditions and attainable level that would cover the budgeted expenditures for the year.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) expedite the updating of taxpayers’ index cards in order to provide the Accounting Office an updated Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due as the basis in setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts; (ii) strictly enforce the remedies for the collection of delinquent RPT as expressly provided in Section 256 of R.A. No. 7160; and (iii) institute appropriate strategies to further improve the collection efficiency; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to use the updated Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due as the basis in recording the RPT/SET tax Receivables at the beginning of the year; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records of RPT/SET Receivables in order to resolve the disparity of ₱64,186,694.06 and to report reliably the said accounts in the financial statements.

For the deficiencies discussed above, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Inventory Committee and Property Officer to (i) conduct complete physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count for CY 2023 and immediately submit the related reports to the Audit Team; and (ii) reconcile the resultant inventory reports with the accounting records; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELCs for the proper control and monitoring of the agency property and equipment; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Property Officer to prioritize the registration of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality with the LTO to prevent possible loss and misuse of these assets and to avoid the incurrence of possible penalties, fines or sanctions from the LTO.

We recommended that the Property Officer facilitate the insurance of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality so that the LGU may be indemnified or compensated for any damage to, or loss due to various casualties that may happen to these insurable assets.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to continue complying with Section 50 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I on (i) classifying and recording PPE as CIP during the construction period and (ii) reclassifying completed projects to appropriate PPE account.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) continue its efforts in the timely implementation of PPAs and full utilization of the funds transferred by National Government Agencies (NGAs); and (b) require the Heads of the Offices concerned to strictly observe the time frame of implementation/completion of the on-going projects for the efficient delivery of public services to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) perform verification and reconciliation of balances of Due to LGU account of the Municipality with the Due from LGU-General Tinio account of the Provincial Government to establish the correct and accurate balances of both accounts; (b) conduct verification, analysis and validation in order to determine the details, whereabouts and validity of the unaccounted balances of Due to LGUs account totaling ₱61,569.39; and (c) maintain complete records of the same.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) LDC through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to (i) strengthen the planning process; and (ii) ensure the viability of all planned projects to maximize fund utilization; (b) Municipal Engineer to (i) closely monitor/supervise project implementation to ensure completion within the contract time for the immediate utilization by the intended beneficiaries; and (ii) strictly adhere  to prescribed regulations in considering requests of contractors for time extension in project completion; (c) Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group to ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in all procurements through public bidding; and (d) OIC-Municipal Accountant to strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of procurements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) LDRRMO, in collaboration with LDRRMC to transmit the lacking documents as discussed above; (b) LDRRMO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to comply with the reporting requirements for LDRRM activities as provided for under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and COA Circular No. 2012-002; (c) Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to establish effective measures expediting the process of DRRM disbursements in order (i) to fix the unexpended QRF and MF – MOOE balances of the current year and (ii) to transfer them to a special trust fund at the end of the year; and (d)  OIC - Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry at year-end to recognize the unexpended balance as Trust Liability – DRRMF in the financial statements as illustrated in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We commended the Management for its unceasing efforts to comply with R.A. No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000. We further recommended that the LCE direct the MENRO to (i) strictly implement mandatory segregation of wastes at source; and (ii) introduce other effective waste disposal methods in addition to its current waste diversion practices to achieve its target and to further reduce residual wastes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) BPLO, Municipal Treasurer and Sangguniang Bayan to (i) closely coordinate and work together for the updating of the Municipal Revenue Code at least every five years, as mandated by law, in order to optimize its revenue generation for the benefit of its constituents; (ii) ensure that the amended Revenue Code reflects the current economic conditions and complies with the Local Government Code of 1991; (b) BPLO to (i) be more strict in the implementation and enforcement of the Municipal Revenue Code; (ii) ensure that the prescribed tax rates are correctly applied based on the line of business of the taxpayer; (iii) use the sworn statement of gross receipts or sales as basis in the initial computation of business tax; and (iv) require the business tax payers to submit a copy of ITRs for the proper computation of deficiency tax, if any; (b) Municipal Treasurer to examine and verify the books of accounts and other pertinent records of any business establishments; and (c) BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to verify, assess, and validate other businesses aside from the noted businesses and demand the payment of computed under collection of business taxes totaling ₱32,660.00.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) person to further improve its compliance on GAD regulations by: (a) vigilantly overseeing the execution of GAD PPAs to ensure their efficient and effective completion within the approved budget and specified timeframe; and (b) utilizing the HGDG gender analysis tool in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Market Administrator to (a) properly and strictly enforce the provisions of the Market Code; (b) issue the necessary follow up/demand letters to the delinquent stallholders for the settlement of their over-due obligations; and (c) enforce revocation or cancellation of contracts of delinquent stallholders who failed to pay the market fees for the period stipulated.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed that the (a) OIC - Municipal Accountant continue complying with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes on compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from government money payments; and (b) OIC - Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer exert extra effort in the processing and timely recording of disbursements in order to fully remit the taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue complying with the R.A. No. 8291 or GSIS Act of 1997 in the withholding of premiums and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to GSIS.

We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue complying with the R.A. No. 9679 or HDMF law of 2009 on the withholding of contributions and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the Municipality’s share and its timely remittances to HDMF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue complying with the R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos. 9241 and 10606 or the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 on the withholding of contributions from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to PHIC.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer require all RCOs to report daily the collections by preparing the RCD at the close of each business day and remit the same intact to her for deposit to the authorized government depository bank.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had set a cut-off daily wherein the RCOs will report their collections daily through RCD prepared by them and remit the same before the close of office hours on the same day, for deposit to the LGU’s depository bank the next working day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer submit the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms to the Audit Team not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month as required in Section 51 of the NGAS for LGUs Manual, Volume II.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant  
(a) timely prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements of all depository accounts so that reconciling items could be determined promptly and adjustments be made in the books; and 
(b) ensure submission of monthly BRS to the Audit Team as required by COA Circular No. 96-011.

observations.managementAction: Liquidated damages amounting to ₱63,513.97 was deducted from the final billing of the contractor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and all concerned Department Heads to 
(a) submit to the Audit Team at the beginning of the year the list of all on-going projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year; and 
(b) ensure that the appropriate signboard containing the information of the project is displayed at the venue where the PPA is located or carried out.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC Chairman, BAC Secretariat and Municipal Health Officer to submit the lacking requirements in the procurement of ancillary supplies and goods and services as required by GPPB Circular No. 02-2021.   
We also recommend that management continue its efforts to promote and administer booster doses in order to maintain constituents' immunity against the COVID-19 virus.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE direct the 
(a) OIC - Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes on compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from government money payments; and 
(b) OIC - Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer exert extra effort in the processing and timely recording of disbursements in order to fully remit the taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer should continue complying with the R.A. No. 8291 or GSIS Act of 1997 in the withholding of premiums and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to GSIS.

We recommended and management agreed that the OIC - Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer should continue complying with the R.A. No. 9679 or HDMF law of 2009 on the withholding of contributions and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to HDMF.

We recommended and the management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer should continue complying with the R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos. 9241 and 10606 or the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 on the withholding of contributions from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to PHIC.

We reiterated the recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
a)	Assign a permanent employee that could focus on the proper recording, issuance, updating and maintenance of Property Cards and records of PPE Accounts;
b)	Require the reconciliation between the results of the actual physical inventory report with the accounting record pursuant to Section   269, Chapter 7, Volume 1, GAM for LGUs, to settle the discrepancy; and
c)	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain the PPELC.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies or materials as an Inventory account pursuant to Sections 188 and 190, Chapter 6, of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the (a) LDRRMO III, in collaboration with LDRRMC, to update the LDRRMP in accordance with Item 3.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1; (b) Chairman, Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Head, BAC Secretariat to (i) ensure that the necessary procurement information or disclosures are timely posted in conspicuous places; and (ii) submit justification on the noted deficiencies in the procurement process and/or transmit the absent or lacking documents as discussed above; (c) OIC – Municipal Accountant to strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of procurements; and (d) LDRRMO III, Local Finance Committee and OIC - Municipal Accountant to comply with the reporting for LDRRM activities as provided for under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Item 4.0 of DBM LBC No. 130.

We recommended and the management agreed that the (a) Acting Municipal Treasurer and OIC - Municipal Accountant to establish effective measures expediting the process of DRRM disbursements in order (i) to fix the unexpended QRF and MF – MOOE balances of the current year and (ii) to transfer them to a special trust fund at the end of the year; and (b) OIC - Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry at year-end to recognize the unexpended balance as Trust Liability – DRRMF in the financial statements as illustrated in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to (a) require all RCO to turn over their collections intact and daily or not later than the next banking day to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; and (b) deposit all collections intact regularly and within the prescribed period to the authorized government depository bank.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the LCE instruct the (a) Acting Municipal Treasurer to (i) institute effective measures to increase tax collection of the Municipality; (ii) on a timely basis, prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due and submit the same to the Accounting Office as the basis in setting-up of RPT/SET receivables in the books of accounts at the beginning of the year; (iii) prepare Certified List of Tax Delinquencies and send notice of delinquencies to the delinquent taxpayers; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to use the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the MTO as the basis in recording the RPT/SET tax receivables at the beginning of the year.

For the deficiencies discussed above, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Inventory Committee and Property Officer to (i) conduct annually the physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count for CY 2021 and immediately submit the related reports to the Audit Team; (ii) reconcile the resultant inventory reports with the Accounting records; and (b) OIC - Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to maintain complete PPE records such as PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards for the proper control and monitoring of the agency property and equipment. We further recommended that the LCE direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in order to render a reliable PPE information.

We recommended and the management agreed that the OIC – Municipal Accountant should:
compute/determine the amount of impairment and provide for allowance for impairment for dormant other receivables account in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statements as required under IPSAS 29 using the following adjusting entries:

comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant other receivables account.

We recommended that the LCE (a) continue its efforts to fully utilize the funds transferred by NGAs for the implementation of  various PPAs; (b) direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate return of the unutilized funds to the respective source agencies in observance of COA Circular No. 94-013; and (c) require the Heads of the Offices concerned to strictly observe the time frame of scheduled implementation/completion of the projects to ensure efficient delivery of public services to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) BAC and TWG to (i) submit the copies of lacking documentary requirements for the above procurements; and (ii) henceforth, ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in all procurements through public bidding; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of procurements; (c) OIC-Municipal Engineer to (i) assess the possibility of the continuation of unimplemented project, otherwise make representation to LDC for its reprogramming to other priority projects; and (ii) closely monitor/supervise project implementation to ensure completion within the contract time for the immediate utilization of the intended beneficiaries; and (d) LDC through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ascertain the viability of each planned project to maximize fund utilization for the benefit of its intended users.

We recommended and the management agreed that the (a) LSB should ensure that (i) SEF expenditures are supported with information that they are net of the DepEd’s budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in its budget; and (ii) the guidelines on SEF operations, budgeting, and utilization are being adhered to; and (iii) submit the lacking documents noted in audit;  (b) Acting Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer should monitor the SEF properties by requesting the Inventory Committee to complete the validation exercise and fix the accountability of the property end-users by requiring them to acknowledge the PAR; and (c) OIC – Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements are supported with necessary documentary requirements.

We commended the management for its unceasing efforts to comply with R.A. No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.  We further recommended and the management agreed that the MENRO should implement alternative waste disposal methods that will further reduce residual wastes.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and OIC – Municipal Accountant to (a) ensure completion of documentary requirements prior to payments of Disbursement Vouchers; (b) exert efforts and strategize effective measures on how to minimize, if not eliminate the incurrence of delays in the submission of accounts; and (c) cause the immediate submission of still untransmitted CY 2021 accounts, records and reports to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer, Human Resource Management Officer III and Municipal Engineer to submit regularly the copies of Contracts and POs together with its supporting documents within the prescribe period.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System, through the GAD Focal Person, (a) continue its efforts to improve gender mainstreaming and compliance with the provisions of GAD laws and regulations; (b) employ the HGDG in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD; and (c) henceforth, aim to attain the full implementation and impose close monitoring of GAD activities indicated in the GPB to fully address the identified gender issues.

We commended the management for its continuous efforts to comply with the COVID-19 vaccination program guidelines, a way of providing protection to their constituents from adverse effects of the virus. We also recommended that the (a) management continue to administer vaccines efficiently so that local herd immunity can be attained in the soonest time possible; and (b) Chairman, BAC and Municipal Health Officer submit the lacking requirements in the procurement of ancillary supplies as discussed above.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed that the (a) OIC - Municipal Accountant should continue complying with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes on compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from government money payments; and (b) OIC - Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer should exert extra effort in the processing and timely recording of disbursements in order to fully remit the taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer should continue complying with the R.A. No. 8291 or GSIS Act of 1997 in the withholding of premiums and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to GSIS.

We recommended and management agreed that the OIC - Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer should continue complying with the R.A. No. 9679 or HDMF law of 2009 on the withholding of contributions and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to HDMF.

We recommended and the management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer should continue complying with the R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos. 9241 and 10606 or the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 on the withholding of contributions from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to PHIC.

We reiterated the recommendations that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the tax collection of the municipality, 



(ii) prepare certified list of all real property tax delinquencies and send notice to the taxpayers, and 



(iii)update the RPT registers; 
b) Municipal Accountant to 
(i) comply with the provision of Section 42, Volume 1 of the GAM for LGUs; and 

(ii) use the Certified List of Taxpayers and amount due prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SET tax receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction:
Per Acting Municipal Treasurer, they will send notices of delinquency to taxpayers after certified list of taxpayers and tax due has been prepared/updated.

The Municipal Treasurer did not furnish the OIC-Municipal Accountant copy of Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due at the beginning of 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) adhere strictly with the provisions of Sections 52 and 54 of RA No. 9184. Resort to procurement thru shopping only if all the conditions enumerated in Section 52 of RA No. 9184 are met and the procurement will not in any way result in splitting of purchase orders;
b) submit an explanation for resorting to splitting; and
c) strictly observe and enforce the rules and regulations pertaining to government procurement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the proper accounting and recording of the unexpended/unobligated balance of the 5% LDRRMF.

We recommended that the that the Local Chief Executive:

a) establish a Gender and Focal Point System (GFPS) pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 to accelerate their gender mainstreaming;

b)  attribute only a portion of the agency’s major programs to the GAD budget by subjecting the program to gender analysis using the HGDG tool; and

c) ensure the complete implementation of activities indicated in the GPB to maximize benefits inuring to the beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive implement the following:

a) Facilitate quickly the inventory taking and the preparation of report in the prescribed forms for submission to GSIS to determine the correct valuation of insurance coverage; and

b) Strictly comply with the requirements of the Property Insurance Law.
We recommended that the Management continue to observe strict compliance with GSIS rules and regulations under Republic Act No. 8291.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to comply strictly with the regulations on withholding taxes on salaries, benefits, and procurement of goods and services and the subsequent remittance thereof pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulations No. 02-1998, Republic Act No. 8424, Section 7 of Revenue Regulations (RR) No. 1-2013 and Section 5 of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2019.

We recommended that the Management continue to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 23 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9679 and Section E.4 of HDMF Circular No. 275 and observe the practice of regular and intact remittance of the monthly employer and employees’ contributions withheld and due to Pag-IBIG.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to adhere strictly with the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014.
